repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 1: shared-edge compatibility makes cellwise bounds nonsharp

published_prior_art.cite: SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026: sharp direct/spillover bounds for three canonical misspecified exposure settings, not shared sparse K-label compatibility

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy/v1 has nonrectangular direct/indirect geometry in mediation, not latent sparse network exposures

published_prior_art.cite: HuLiWager2022: specified-exposure direct/indirect decomposition, not latent-map joint nonrectangularity

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 2: capacitated-incidence certificate characterization for fixed hidden-edge ADE endpoints

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 + EdmondsMatchingPolytope1965 + Wolsey1975 give component machinery, not the combined sparse K-label network endpoint certificate

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: Section 4 has five substantive paragraphs; the required six subparagraphs are not separately present, with community implications and research-benefit framing merged.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The cellwise relaxation is not specified enough: theta_DI(Z,Y), active masks, support-size constraints, and E_cell are invoked for fractional Z_iak without a precise polytope or integer/fractional convention.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch names Edmonds and Wolsey but does not give a concrete reduction from general K-support exposure-label endpoint rejection to the claimed exhaustive two-type certificate family.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the cellwise relaxation as an explicit finite polytope or mixed-integer set, including denominators, active labels, and the map from Z to D_k and I_kell.
Separate the Introduction into the six required subparagraphs and add a one-sentence research-benefit paragraph distinct from applied community implications.
For Conjecture 3, state the exact K=2 certificate theorem first, then make the general K certificate a separate conjecture with the specific integer-programming certificate class named.
Sharpen the repo-axis distinction from pid_network_exposure/v1 by explicitly stating the new degrees of freedom introduced by latent K-support labels and joint direct/indirect directions.

recommended_next_step: Patch the cellwise-relaxation definition and Conjecture 3 proof route, then split the Introduction into the required six-paragraph structure.
